79104.name: Dry Riverbed
79104.description: To call the River Dreck a river any longer is a little misleading. In fact, the so-called river is the exact opposite - a parched gulf cutting through the countryside, without a trace of water. Such is the arid composition of the riverbed that its cessation could not have been caused by natural processes. It is as if some powerful magical force has torn the liquid and moisture of the Dreck right out of existence.

79105.name: Dry Riverbed
79105.description: Small trees line the banks of the River Dreck here. Although the life- giving waters of the stream are long gone, the foliage here maintains a healthy hue, as if eternally preserved by some form of paranormal nutrition. An ancient shack rises out of the woods, blocking your exit to the north, but no door is visible from this angle.

79106.name: Dry Riverbed
79106.description: The slim lining of brush and trees around the riverbed is not particularly thick or extensive, yet it feels as though it acutely isolates and engulfs the area, breaking it free from time and space. Drifting faintly on the breeze, you think you hear faint bursts of abrasive noise in the distance - the baying of hounds, perhaps? It must just be your imagination.

79107.name: Sub-Mareldjan Meadowland
79107.description: Probably once a device helping to feed the mouths of Mareldja's public, this broad, featureless plain is not currently in use. Thick blades of grass sway in the gentle breezes with the natural dignity derived from the prolonged absence of mankind. The air is salty and has a certain marine chill to it, though the latter is not particularly acute.

79108.name: Dry Riverbed
79108.description: A long time ago, the waters of the River Dreck would have carried this debris into the Chardaleth Sea, but they no longer do. For countless generations the river has maintained utter inaction in natural eulogy to Thurvun's most notorious servant.

79109.name: Dry Riverbed
79109.description: The arid channel marking the former passage of the Dreck winds through the countryside south of Mareldja here. A few trees, small yet clearly gnarled with age line the riverbank, growing denser and somewhat larger to the west.

79110.name: Meadow by the Sea
79110.description: A broad area of unmaintained turf slopes rapidly towards the sands to the south, the grasses fresh and obviously unaccustomed to pedestrian movement, evidenced by their pristine condition. The ground is strikingly green as a result, almost unnaturally so.

79111.name: Steep Meadow
79111.description: The land here inclines acutely southward, straight towards the Chardaleth Sea. Deciduous trees, mostly oaks, are scattered here and there, increasing both in quantity and maturity the further north they are. It would seem that over the centuries, acorns jettisoned from their boughs have rolled down the steep hill, resulting in the younger generations growing closer to the bottom, their ancestors aging at the summit.

79112.name: The Woods
79112.description: Deciduous branches, gnarly with age, bend under their own weight towards the path. Moss adorns the more senior trees, peaceful symbiosis taking place over the eons. These trees live their lives in slow motion, but careful reflection on their current configuration can reveal the goings on of their existence, the most trivial of which have taken place over at least the space of a human lifetime.

79113.name: The Woods
79113.description: Through the trunks of the oaks which now surround you, you catch a glimpse of the Chardaleth to the west, and you can feel the cool ocean breeze rustle into the earth leads through this small copse.

79114.name: The Woods
79114.description: Weary boughs push themselves upward, reaching for the sky and the nourishing sunlight it brings. The trees here seem almost to have a hunger, not menacing in the slightest, but with a certain sense of urgency. Their roots are plunged into the soft earth like the digits of emaciated men delving into a pie or cake, as if the fertility of the ground here is almost temporary and the trees are aware, to the maximum extent that trees can be aware, of this fact.

79115.name: Mouth of the Brown River
79115.description: The estuary to your west marks the point where the Brown River enters the coastal waters of the Chardaleth Sea. Expelled rapidly from moist jungle, the current gradually slows as it dissipates into the ocean. Centuries of erosion by the river have cut a wide bay into the coastline, flanked by dual peninsulae many leagues apart.

79116.name: Before Cliffs
79116.description: Baranath Bay meets its eastern boundary here - the impassible cliffs of the west coast rise with immediacy from the deep water without any sort of landing point. The water is a surreal, dark cyan beneath you, utterly clear without sand or earth to cloud it, yet darkened by the proximity of the rock face.

79117.name: Before Cliffs
79117.description: Cold water, lapping in from the west, crashes with considerable momentum against an anomalous section of cliff which protrudes from the rest. This point is close to the middle of the great bay of Baranath on the west coast, and as such is more vulnerable to the onslaught of the tides than the rest of the bay's shoreline.

79118.name: Shingle Cove
79118.description: An area of shingle not submerged rises beside the cliffs here, just wide enough to stand on. The pebbles closest to the water glisten as they are periodically sprayed, while next to the cliff they are dryer and coated in dust fallen from the clifftops above.

79119.name: Shingle Cove
79119.description: The consistency of the rock which makes up the cliffs in this southeastern corner of Baranath Bay has encouraged gentle erosion and formed a beach of small stones, washed perfectly smooth by the never ending motion of the ocean as it laps in and out. Passage south is blocked by a cliff face - the peninsula it leads into marks the southern boundary of the bay - but travel is possible around it to the west, through the water. Miles to the north, a much larger peninsula is visible.

79120.name: Southern End of Baranath Bay
79120.description: An extended spit of rocky cliff, barring your way to the south and east, marks the southern end of Baranath Bay. The nooks and crannies of the rocks above are an ideal hideaway for animals blessed with the ability of flight, and it would appear that a large colony of sea birds have nested within the cliffs.

79121.name: Baranath Bay
79121.description: The water here is very calm, and the reduced movement makes it a little warmer. The tiniest of ripples form towards the east, where it becomes shallower and a bank of shingle rises from below.

79122.name: Baranath Bay
79122.description: The western vista here is, as stunningly beautiful as the vast Chardaleth Sea is, somewhat unremarkable in the sense that it is completely uniform. Beyond a certain distance, individual waves can no longer be made out, resulting in an unchanging blanket of blue. At much greater proximity to the east, a secluded shingle cove rises before the cliffs, bringing back some sense of scale.

79123.name: Baranath Bay
79123.description: You lie approximately in the middle of Baranath Bay, and as a result of this the water is far choppier than to the north and south. The twin peninsulas which contain the bay offer minimal protection from the raw battery of the Chardaleth Sea at this point.

79124.name: Baranath Bay
79124.description: Who would have guessed that the filth of the Brown River could, after centuries of running its natural course towards the Chardaleth, create a scene so magnificent? In this glistening bay, all traces of the muck for which the river is famous have dissipated, leaving the clean waters to carve the picturesque beaches and rock faces visible on each side of the bay.

79125.name: Baranath Bay
79125.description: A current pushes westward, gentle but firm, inland waters eager to connect once again with the primordial depths of the Chardaleth Sea from whence they originated. The sparsity of wildlife in the bay is immediately noticable, it would appear that the pickings for the airborne scavengers traditional to the coastal regions is slim here, perhaps due to the constant seaward motion of the water.

79126.name: The Open Sea
79126.description: To your east and north, the vast waters of Baranath Bay sprawl wide, blue and deep. You become aware of your relative distance from the shore, but mercifully a rocky peninsula immediately to your southeast allows you to get your bearings. It can be approached to your east or south, any other movement would send you onto the unfathomable high seas.

79127.name: Before the Southern Peninsula
79127.description: Rising before you to your west, a monolithic natural structure of rock forcefully juts out from the rest of the coastline, utterly insurmountable for its height and sharp gradient. You heard the falsetto squawking of infant gulls from high above, but they and their parents are out of sight. The exposure of this stretch of water makes the waves a good deal rougher, and you bob uncontrollably as they rapidly advance and recede.

79131.name: The Open Sea
79131.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79132.name: The Open Sea
79132.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79133.name: The Open Sea
79133.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79134.name: The Open Sea
79134.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79135.name: The Open Sea
79135.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79136.name: The Open Sea
79136.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79137.name: The Open Sea
79137.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79138.name: The Open Sea
79138.description: You are far from the sight of land. The endless expanse of water and the constant tossing of the waves make it impossible to move in a straight line. If fortune smiles on you, it may be that your wandering will bring you closer to the coast.

79140.name: Outer Baranath Bay
79140.description: Bobbing about in the outermost waters of Baranath Bay, the briny gales of the open ocean whistle about you in every direction. Besides the headland directly to your south, you can make out no other useful landmark. Its austere breadth, as it pans southwesterly out to its tip in the freezing Chardaleth Sea beyond, serves as the only visual aid to navigation here. Lose sight of it, and you may have difficulty returning to the mainland.

79141.name: The Open Sea
79141.description: You have left Baranath Bay and now float dangerously far from the coast. You can still make out the cliffs on the eastern horizon, but it would be unwise to travel any further away from them.

79142.name: The Open Sea
79142.description: You have left Baranath Bay and now float dangerously far from the coast. You can still make out the cliffs on the eastern horizon, but it would be unwise to travel any further away from them.

79143.name: The Open Sea
79143.description: You have left Baranath Bay and now float dangerously far from the coast. You can still make out the cliffs on the eastern horizon, but it would be unwise to travel any further away from them.

79144.name: The Open Sea
79144.description: The coast is still visible both to the north and the east, but you are fairly far from it and any further seaward travel could land you in trouble. The currents are strong out here, the waves rough and the wind brutal.

79091.name: North of the Estuary
79091.description: The ground here is marshy, saturated with the water of the Brown River, whose mouth lies to your south, the fresh and salt waters mingling and floating out to sea. The Brown River is fresh water only in the sense that it is free from salt - the sludge and Oldgate sewage from which it derives its moniker are still clearly visible, even this far downstream. Your way to the east is blocked by the thick undergrowth from which the river ejects, but open countryside dotted with occasional groves of trees rolls northward, and in the distance you see the faint outlines of a large settlement - most likely Marledja.

79092.name: Small Beach
79092.description: The solid turf here is very close to the water, leaving scope for only a small stretch of sand. The sea before you has a definite westward motion - some river to the east pours its contents into the ocean, and the tide is not strong enough to act against this constant expulsion.

79093.name: Mouth of the River Dreck
79093.description: The beach here is covered in dried streaks of mud, leading towards the water from a dry riverbed cutting through the ground to the north. Clearly, a small river once met the ocean here, but for some reason it has since ceased to carry water. Such is the curvature of the beach that travel any further west will land you in the water, but you could easily follow the riverbed as it winds northward into the fields.

79094.name: Shallow Coastal Water
79094.description: The waters of the Chardaleth Sea, incalculably immense, spread before your eyes to the south and to the west. The total absence of land for miles increases the dominance of the sky in your field of vision, rendering epic cloud structures completely visible, shifting and rolling in mighty, endless dance above the shimmering depths.

79095.name: Deep Coastal Water
79095.description: From this angle, the true magnitude of the Chardaleth Sea is brought into sharp perspective. The main bulk of the coastline is barely visible, and unbroken ocean fills your vision in most directions. The one identifiable landmark, a long peninsula isolated from the rest of the continent stands above the depths, its rocky body preventing northward travel. Pristine meadowland stretches atop it, but it is out of reach from here.

79096.name: Deep Coastal Water
79096.description: The spit of land to your north stretches immense into the sky above you as you edge along its rocky base. Rough waves periodically career from the west with extreme force as they wash in from the open waters of the Chardaleth, meeting the cliffs with dangerous velocity.

79097.name: The Open Sea
79097.description: The mainland is almost invisible to you, sinking below the eastern horizon many miles away. You are able to keep your bearings only in relation to the great headland immediately to your north.

79098.name: The Open Sea
79098.description: The only land you can see is the peninsula to your northeast, and even that is receding into the distance. It would be wise to approach it to the north or to the east rather than to risk getting lost at sea.

79099.name: West of the Peninsula
79099.description: A spit of land rises out of the water to the east, red earth topped high above with lush grasses. It is impassible for its height, leaving only watery routes in all other directions. To the west, the measureless sea roars forebodingly and without mercy.

79100.name: West of the Peninsula
79100.description: The water here is shallow as part of the peninsula to your east slowly descends into the ocean, yet no actual landing is possible due to the steep gradient to the top. In all other directions, water laps in dull repetition.

79101.name: The Open Sea
79101.description: Out of sight of the mainland, your only marker is the rocky headland to your southeast. No landing seems possible from this angle, though presumably the long peninsula meets the mainland at some point.

79102.name: Wooded Hill
79102.description: Tall oaks, knotted and wrinkled pillars of bark, rise out of the meadowland marking the edge of a small wood to the north. Descendant saplings surround you, not yet worn through decades of existence. Beneath your feet, roots exit and re-enter the soft earth, some shimmering and polished by the passage of animals and men, some still rough and undamaged.

79103.name: Dry Riverbed
79103.description: Pebbles and other debris carried from further inland are mummified here in the dried mud. No trace of the water which once ran through here can today be found, and it is clear that the stream has been out of action for quite some time, leaving this channel of caked earth and stones its only epitaph.

79145.name: Balcony
79145.description: A clean deck of planks adorns the cliff summit here, untidy limbs of foliage pushed back and recently cleared with meticulous care. Heather spills over the precipice to your west, and beyond it an impressive view of the coast sprawls towards the horizon. Sea birds soar across the waves hunting for underwater meals, some appearing as no more than white specks due to their extreme distance. Wide glass doors meet the balcony immediately to the east.

79146.name: Conservatory
79146.description: The walls of this room, which are for the most part transparent, cause the tiled floor, the furniture and the air to warm considerably, as brilliant sunlight permeates it throughout the daytime. The glass is impeccably clean, and the glorious western marine vista is plainly visible.

79147.name: Fidel's Apartment
79147.description: The success of this notorious mobster's primary business venture manifests itself considerably in the affluent decor of his property. A thick, exotic carpet seperates your feet from the polished wooden floor, and the otherwise minimally decorated walls to your east and west showcase expensive artwork bought, or more likely plundered, by McGee during his ongoing smuggling exploits.

79148.name: Fidel's Study
79148.description: A surprising quantity of literature is on display in this room, lining a wide shelf against the western wall as well as smaller cases arranged in a neat circle around the low, transparent table in the centre of the room. A wide window in the southern wall looks out over the clifftops, curtains undulating gently in the cool, moist breeze.

79089.name: Granite Headland
79089.description: Intense wind races wildly over the speckled grey rocks to which you cling for dear life, and the violent ocean beneath you thunders like a wild animal as it throws itself against the cliff. Such conditions have done little to deter the inhabitants of this barren natural fortress however - rugged gulls have built an array of sturdy nests in which to rear their young at a safe distance from predation.
